channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: DeepSeek V4 may launch this month, test interface suggests Vision and Expert modes
item.dc:creator: Jessie Wu
item.description: In China’s domestic large model sector, DeepSeek V4 is undoubtedly one of the most highly anticipated releases in recent months. Since the launch of its test version in February, users have been eagerly awaiting the official release. Coupled with a recent surge in system crashes and anomalies, the market speculates that these may be stress […]
item.content:encoded:
In China’s domestic large model sector, DeepSeek V4 is undoubtedly one of the most highly anticipated releases in recent months. Since the launch of its test version in February, users have been eagerly awaiting the official release.

Coupled with a recent surge in system crashes and anomalies, the market speculates that these may be stress tests ahead of the new version’s rollout, with a full launch expected as early as this month. A recently surfaced screenshot from a gray-scale test has further fueled expectations of a major upgrade.

A well-known programmer on Chinese social platform Weibo, @Yigongchang, on Tuesday shared a screenshot of DeepSeek’s updated interface. Unlike the current version, which features the familiar dual options of Deep Thinking (R1) and Smart Search (online), the new interface clearly shows three additional modes: Fast, Expert, and Vision.

This shift signals a redesign of the interaction logic and points directly to a more segmented product lineup. Analysts believe the Fast mode may correspond to a lightweight model, while Expert is likely tied to a deep-reasoning model.

The prominent inclusion of a Vision option further indicates that the previously reportedly multimodal capabilities are now confirmed, marking a crucial step for DeepSeek in closing the gap with top-tier large model competitors.

Compared with the current version, DeepSeek’s upcoming release may not be a single model, but rather a suite of offerings. Based on the interface options, the new versions could be named DeepSeek V4 Lite (Fast version), DeepSeek V4 (Expert version), and DeepSeek V4 Vision (Vision version).

Previous reports have indicated that DeepSeek is developing at least three large models and is building them on a domestic AI chip-based computing platform, making the information from this gray-scale test highly consistent with earlier leaks.

Although DeepSeek has yet to reveal specific upgrade details, the release of the V4 series appears imminent. Looking back at last year, the launch of DeepSeek R1 shook the market because it had a high cost-performance ratio. It even briefly impacted NVIDIA’s market value.

Now, with the V4 series completing its multimodal capabilities, domestic expectations are high. Some industry voices are even calling for a special version aimed at AI programming, positioning it to directly compete with Anthropic’s Claude series in coding capabilities.

Whether DeepSeek can once again leverage its technological strength to shake up the market and put pressure on Anthropic or OpenAI remains to be seen—answers may emerge in the coming days.

item.title: Amazon to cut 14,000 jobs globally, China operations may face deep impact
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Amazon plans to launch a new round of global layoffs in May 2026, with about 14,000 employees expected to be affected. The cuts will span several core business units, including AWS cloud services, retail, and human resources, while some teams in China could face complete shutdowns. The most controversial aspect of the layoffs is that […]
item.content:encoded:
Amazon plans to launch a new round of global layoffs in May 2026, with about 14,000 employees expected to be affected. The cuts will span several core business units, including AWS cloud services, retail, and human resources, while some teams in China could face complete shutdowns. The most controversial aspect of the layoffs is that the selection process is reportedly not based on objective criteria such as performance or projects, but instead involves managers making random picks, leaving affected employees with little to no channel for appeal.

This lottery-style approach has caused significant anxiety among staff. The layoffs are said to primarily target white-collar employees and mid-level managers, particularly those at levels L5 to L7, while frontline blue-collar roles in areas such as warehousing and logistics are not currently within the scope of the cuts. [Kuai Technology, in Chinese]

item.title: Foxconn begins trial production of Apple’s first foldable iPhone, expected to be its priciest ever
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Foxconn has begun trial production of Apple’s first foldable iPhone, sources familiar with the matter said, with the device expected to launch in the second half of 2026. The phone will feature a horizontal “book-style” foldable design, a 7.7–8 inch 4K-level inner display, and an outer screen with a punch-hole front camera. Apple has combined […]
item.content:encoded:
Foxconn has begun trial production of Apple’s first foldable iPhone, sources familiar with the matter said, with the device expected to launch in the second half of 2026. The phone will feature a horizontal “book-style” foldable design, a 7.7–8 inch 4K-level inner display, and an outer screen with a punch-hole front camera. Apple has combined its Vision Pro display team with the iPhone hardware group to tackle foldable screen challenges, promising a crease-free display and a folding lifespan of 300,000 cycles.

The China version is expected to start at 14,000–15,000 yuan ($1,960–2,100), while the top-tier model could exceed 20,000 yuan ($2,800), making it the most expensive iPhone ever. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: Honor to unveil HONOR WIN gaming laptop next month in push back into high-end segment
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese tech firm Honor said on Tuesday it will launch its new HONOR WIN gaming laptop on April 23, marking its return to the high-end gaming notebook segment. The model will focus on both performance and thermal efficiency, and will compete with Lenovo’s Legion Y9000P Supreme Edition. According to sources, the top-end version is expected […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese tech firm Honor said on Tuesday it will launch its new HONOR WIN gaming laptop on April 23, marking its return to the high-end gaming notebook segment. The model will focus on both performance and thermal efficiency, and will compete with Lenovo’s Legion Y9000P Supreme Edition. According to sources, the top-end version is expected to feature an Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor and an NVIDIA RTX 5070 graphics card, with system power exceeding 250 watts.

It will also include a proprietary cooling system designed to ensure stable performance under heavy workloads. Honor added that the HONOR WIN gaming laptop has been selected as the official device for the professional league of Delta Force, a popular tactical first-person shooter game published by Tencent. [TechNode Reporting]

item.title: Doubao surpasses 120 trillion daily tokens as usage doubles in three months
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: According to Volcano Engine, the Seedance 2.0 API has entered public beta for enterprise users. As of March this year, Doubao—an AI large model developed by ByteDance—has seen its daily token usage exceed 120 trillion, doubling over the past three months and increasing 1,000-fold compared to its launch in May 2024. Currently, the number of […]
item.content:encoded:
According to Volcano Engine, the Seedance 2.0 API has entered public beta for enterprise users. As of March this year, Doubao—an AI large model developed by ByteDance—has seen its daily token usage exceed 120 trillion, doubling over the past three months and increasing 1,000-fold compared to its launch in May 2024. Currently, the number of companies on Volcano Engine with cumulative token usage exceeding one trillion has grown from 100 at the end of last year to 140.

Tan Dai, President of Volcano Engine, stated that token usage is a key indicator of AI development speed. The recent rapid growth in Doubao’s usage has been primarily driven by the surge in AI video creation and the accelerated adoption of AI agents. [Jiwei, in Chinese]

item.title: ByteDance short drama platform responds to AI “face theft” claims, removes series over unauthorized face use
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: ByteDance’s short drama platform, Hongguo, has responded to allegations that the AI-generated series Peach Blossom Hairpin was involved in unauthorized facial likeness use. The platform said that after receiving a complaint, it conducted a 72-hour review and found that the producer failed to provide evidence of compliant use of the materials, determining the case to […]
item.content:encoded:
ByteDance’s short drama platform, Hongguo, has responded to allegations that the AI-generated series Peach Blossom Hairpin was involved in unauthorized facial likeness use. The platform said that after receiving a complaint, it conducted a 72-hour review and found that the producer failed to provide evidence of compliant use of the materials, determining the case to constitute a violation of platform rules and contractual obligations.

Hongguo subsequently removed the series in full and suspended the producer from uploading any new content for 15 days. The platform added that it will continue to strengthen its content review mechanisms, enhance technical capabilities, and improve authorization verification processes to protect rights holders and support a healthy industry ecosystem.

The controversy had previously drawn widespread attention after the series was found to have used an individual’s likeness without authorization to generate a negative character. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: Doubao 2 AI smartphone tipped for Q2 debut, two top Chinese phone makers in talks
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: The second-generation Doubao AI smartphone is expected to launch in the second quarter of this year, according to tech bloggers, as ByteDance deepens its push into system-level AI on mobile devices. The device continues ByteDance’s partnership with ZTE’s Nubia brand and will focus on system-level AI and cross-application autonomous operations, aiming to enable a new […]
item.content:encoded:
The second-generation Doubao AI smartphone is expected to launch in the second quarter of this year, according to tech bloggers, as ByteDance deepens its push into system-level AI on mobile devices. The device continues ByteDance’s partnership with ZTE’s Nubia brand and will focus on system-level AI and cross-application autonomous operations, aiming to enable a new interaction model in which AI performs tasks on behalf of users, sources said. Two of China’s top five smartphone makers have begun talks with the Doubao team, with potential collaborations likely to center on deep system integration, the bloggers said.

An earlier prototype faced restrictions from major apps due to permission issues. For the second-generation model, ByteDance has reached partial agreements with Alibaba-affiliated platforms to open permissions in certain scenarios, though it remains unclear whether it can overcome barriers from dominant ecosystems such as WeChat. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Xiaomi’s CEO showcases new-gen SU7 in teardown livestream, says orders exceed 40,000
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Lei Jun, founder of Xiaomi, hosted a livestream that lasted more than three hours at the company’s auto factory in Beijing’s Yizhuang district and offered a detailed teardown of the new-generation SU7 sedan. During the broadcast, Lei, alongside automotive safety expert Zhu Xichan, examined the vehicle component by component, covering exterior paintwork, forged wheels, interior […]
item.content:encoded:
Lei Jun, founder of Xiaomi, hosted a livestream that lasted more than three hours at the company’s auto factory in Beijing’s Yizhuang district and offered a detailed teardown of the new-generation SU7 sedan. During the broadcast, Lei, alongside automotive safety expert Zhu Xichan, examined the vehicle component by component, covering exterior paintwork, forged wheels, interior materials and a 7.1.4-channel audio system, as well as structural elements such as aerospace-grade aluminum anti-collision beams and battery safety tests, including high-temperature nail penetration trials.

Lei said locked orders for the new SU7 had exceeded 40,000 units. Since deliveries began on March 23, the company has delivered more than 7,000 vehicles within nine days, averaging nearly 800 units per day, he added. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: Stellantis reportedly in talks with Leapmotor over Canada EV plant
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Stellantis is reportedly discussing a potential EV project with Leapmotor for its idled Brampton plant in Canada. The talks are still preliminary, and no decision has been made. The Brampton site had previously been set to build a new Jeep SUV, but Stellantis later canceled that plan and moved the program to a US factory. […]
item.content:encoded:
Stellantis is reportedly discussing a potential EV project with Leapmotor for its idled Brampton plant in Canada. The talks are still preliminary, and no decision has been made.

The Brampton site had previously been set to build a new Jeep SUV, but Stellantis later canceled that plan and moved the program to a US factory. Since then, the company has been discussing future options for the plant with Canadian officials.

Leapmotor is already a Stellantis partner. Stellantis bought a 20% stake in the Chinese EV maker in 2023, and the two companies later set up Leapmotor International for overseas production and sales. Canada’s industry ministry said any new auto investment should prioritize local labor and parts suppliers, while Stellantis said all options remain on the table for Brampton. [Bloomberg]

item.title: OpenClaw launches official China mirror with infrastructure support from ByteDance
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: OpenClaw announced on April 1 that it has launched an official China mirror for ClawHub, allowing users to search for skills through a localized access point aimed at improving stability and access speed for users in China. According to the site description, the mirror is a localized skills site built on top of ClawHub and […]
item.content:encoded:
OpenClaw announced on April 1 that it has launched an official China mirror for ClawHub, allowing users to search for skills through a localized access point aimed at improving stability and access speed for users in China.

According to the site description, the mirror is a localized skills site built on top of ClawHub and supported by infrastructure from ByteDance. The notice also said ByteDance only provides technical support for the mirror service and does not make any express or implied guarantees or assume responsibility for its availability. [TechNode reporting]
